question.text: A Fund Factsheet is a report published by mutual funds on a:

answer.text: Monthly basis, disclosing scheme portfolios and returns relative to the benchmark
Daily basis, showing the NAV and AUM of the scheme
Yearly basis, disclosing the audited financial statements of the scheme
Quarterly basis, showing only the top 5 holdings of the scheme

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** A Fund Factsheet is a monthly report made available to investors. It discloses the scheme's objective, category, plan options, AUM, benchmark, minimum investment amount, exit load, Riskometer, fund manager details, portfolio allocation, top 10 holdings, sector-wise allocation, historical performance data, and key ratios.
**The Trap:** NAV is disclosed daily, not through fact sheets. Audited financials are disclosed annually/half-yearly. The fact sheet is monthly and comprehensive, covering far more than just top 5 holdings — it includes top 10 holdings, sector allocation, and much more.
**Cross-Exam Context:** MF topics overlap with Series V-A, V-B. Understanding the various disclosure documents and their frequency is important across all MF exams.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Make fact sheets your go-to tool for client meetings. They provide a concise yet comprehensive view of the fund — performance, portfolio, and risk — all in one document. Review them monthly to stay updated on your recommended funds.
